without_skill_failures: The code calls .AddSaml() on the IdentityServer builder chain
No separate NuGet package is added — SAML is built into Duende.IdentityServer
A SamlServiceProvider is created with EntityId set to 'https://crm.contoso.com'
AssertionConsumerServiceUrls contains 'https://crm.contoso.com/saml/acs'
The code mentions or uses AddInMemorySamlServiceProviders to register the SP
Implements ISamlServiceProviderStore interface
The store class has a FindByEntityIdAsync method that accepts a string entityId and CancellationToken
Uses AddSamlServiceProviderStore<T>() for registration
Implements ISamlClaimsMapper interface
The mapper returns SamlAttribute objects with custom Name values
Warns that ISamlClaimsMapper completely replaces default claim mapping — DefaultClaimMappings are not applied
Registers the custom mapper with dependency injection (e.g., AddTransient)
Mentions DefaultClockSkew as the relevant setting
Shows configuration via options.Saml.DefaultClockSkew
Does NOT suggest changes to the OIDC/OAuth configuration — this is SAML-specific
Mentions that the IdP-initiated SSO endpoint must be enabled via options.Endpoints.EnableSamlIdpInitiatedSsoEndpoint = true
Notes that SignAssertion is the default and most interoperable option
Warns that DoNotSign should only be used for testing, never in production
States that no separate NuGet package is needed — SAML is built into Duende.IdentityServer
States that Enterprise Edition license is required
Mentions SAML support was introduced in version 8.0
Uses ISamlInteractionService injected via dependency injection
Calls GetRequestContextAsync with the returnUrl parameter
Checks if the returned context is non-null to determine if it's a SAML request
Accesses context.ServiceProvider or context.RequestedNameIdFormat for SP details
